text: When working on a project with Git, branching is an essential part of the workflow. A feature branch allows you to develop new functionality separately from the main codebase, reducing the risk of breaking stable code. Once your work is complete, you can merge the changes back into the main branch. This is often done through a pull request, which lets team members review the code before integration. This structured approach keeps the version control workflow organized and ensures that only tested, approved updates are added to the project’s history.
